Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Family Life Development Center records
New York State College of Human Ecology. Family Life Development Center
Cornell University. Iscol Family Program for Leadership Development in Public Service
23-13-2854
21.9 cubic feet. 21.9 cubic feet.
1974-2010. Manuals, videotapes, DVDs, CDs, booklets, manuscripts, dolls Collection material in English 2891958
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Eileen Keating
Date completed:
Nov. 10, 2003
EAD encoding:

Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, November 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Historical information, guides and manuals, booklets, teaching material, curricula, and drafts of a text on family life; includes five anatomical dolls formerly used in child abuse cases, and articles representing opposing opinions on the use of dolls and material from the Child Protective Services Training Institute. Also, videtapes of Professor James Garbarino appearing on television discussing youth and guns.
Also seven DVDs and one leaflet relating to the conference: The Immigrant Child: Past, Present and Future which was put on by the Family Life Development Center in the College of Human Ecology in October of 2008. Joan Jacobs Brumberg was the project director.
Also 13 DVDs of various presentations, including the Iscol Family Program for Leadership Development in Public Service.
Includes records and 10 CDs concerning the Family Life Development Center Military Projects.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Articles and Papers Concerning FLDC Boxes: Box 2, folders 1, 5; Box 3, folder 1
Series II. Curriculum
Subseries A: Handbooks and ManualsBoxes: Box 1, folders 1-2, 5-6, 8, 26-27; Box 2, folder 2
Subseries B: Child Protective Services Training Institute (1971-2003)Boxes: Box 2, folder 6; Boxes 6-22
Series III. Reports/Proposals/Subject Files Boxes: Box 1, folders 4, 7; Box 2, folders 7-8; Box 3, folders 2-3
Series IV. Reviews Boxes: Box 1, folders 9-24; Box 3, folders 4-6
Series V. National Data Archive on Child Abuse and Neglect Box 25
Series VI. The Military Projects Box 26, Box 27
Subseries A: Army Family Advocacy Program Box 26
Subseries B: Various Advocacy ProgramsBox 27
Series VII. Dolls Used in Counseling Sessions Boxes: 4-5
Series VIII. Conferences and Presentations Box: 23
Subseries A: Iscol Family Program for Leadership Development in Public Service
Subseries B: John Doris Lectures
General
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/8130
